run_summary: Screen 1 faces a portfolio already deep in the Anthropic Claude Fable 5 / Gemini sympathy-fade thesis (6 positions, 8-6 days held, mixed P&L) while today's fresh flags span two new trigger events (Google Gemini smart home, Android 17, Meta AI Mode, Apple AI Siri). Portfolio positions remain intact on thesis; new flags cluster heavily (12+ names from 3-4 distinct triggers), triggering basket-rule discipline: most new BUYs default to exploratory tier, with conviction reserved only for names with materially sharper filings evidence and threat-free readings.

entries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Google Home consumer speaker has zero overlap with municipal photo-enforcement contracts, court-admissible chain of custody, or the physical camera infrastructure VRRM operates. The sharp drop on light volume looks like indiscriminate AI-basket selling.
entries.what_kills: News of a municipality piloting AI-only enforcement (no physical cameras); a key Commercial Services customer non-renewal (the 10-Q flagged one significant CS customer in negotiation); Ontario-style legislative bans spreading.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings evidence is crisp: municipal photo-enforcement under multi-year DOT contracts (NYCDOT ~17.9% revenue) with physical camera infrastructure and court-admissible chain of custody. Google Home smart speaker has zero mechanical overlap. Move is -6.18% on 0.52x volume (light), five-day change +0.64% suggests sharp single-day overshoot. Basket rule applies: this is one of 12+ Google Gemini/Meta/Android flags firing today. Even though threat_assessment='none' (conviction-tier-eligible), the basket contains 3+ names from the same Gemini trigger (VRRM, MANH, BLKB, OSPN, TYL, PLAB, ONTO, AEIS, DOCN all from Gemini Home or Android 17), so basket rule forces exploratory tier. 6% sizing.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes the 0.52x volume and -6.18% move are purely sympathy-driven, but provides no evidence ruling out legitimate news flow specific to VRRM's municipal customer base or contract renewal risks.
red_team.critique: The thesis rests on zero mechanical overlap between Google Home and photo-enforcement, which is sound. However, the bull case acknowledges NYCDOT represents ~17.9% of revenue and the 10-Q flagged one significant Commercial Services customer in negotiation—these are material tail risks that the 'indiscriminate basket selling' framing may obscure. The five-day change of +0.64% is positive, but the 1-day drop of -6.18% on light volume (0.52x) could reflect legitimate municipal customer sentiment unrelated to AI news. The bull case does not address whether munis are accelerating digital transformation or if any VRRM customer has signaled a shift toward AI-native enforcement models.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: Google Home speakers don't compete with enterprise warehouse management systems. MANH's moat is multi-year deployments integrating with physical warehouse hardware and ERP systems — switching cost is the moat. Already extended over five days, suggesting broad SaaS de-rating rather than name-specific impairment.
entries.what_kills: A hyperscaler announcing an AI-native WMS replacement; a Tier-1 retailer publicly migrating off MANH to an LLM-orchestrated supply chain platform; further extension of the 5-day decline on rising volume.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings evidence addresses the right risk: generative AI as internal operational risk, not as replacement for enterprise WMS. Core moat is multi-year deployments, ERP integration, physical warehouse hardware switching costs. Google Home is orthogonal. Move is -5.24% on 1.24x volume (elevated), five-day decline -9.0% suggests broader SaaS de-rating rather than name-specific impairment. Basket rule: same Gemini trigger cluster, forcing exploratory tier despite confidence=4 and minimal threat. 6% sizing.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case attributes the -9.0% five-day decline to 'broad SaaS de-rating' rather than name-specific impairment, but does not verify this with comparable SaaS peer moves or explain why MANH's decline would be larger than the basket average if it were truly indiscriminate.
red_team.critique: The thesis that Google Home has zero overlap with enterprise WMS is correct. However, the five-day decline of -9.0% on elevated volume (1.24x) is material and the bull case's attribution to 'broader SaaS de-rating' is asserted but not substantiated. If MANH is down 9% and the broader SaaS sector is down 3-5%, that delta suggests a name-specific risk (e.g., a customer or competitive announcement) rather than sympathy fade. The bull case does not address whether any large retailers or 3PLs have announced AI-native supply-chain initiatives that would pressure MANH's competitive position over the next 12 months.

entries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker doesn't replace nonprofit fundraising / donor management workflows with embedded payment processing and 20+ years of historical donor data. Blackbaud's own filings explicitly warn that AI sentiment can drive stock volatility independent of fundamentals — this is that pattern.
entries.what_kills: A nonprofit-focused AI startup gaining material traction with enterprise nonprofits; renewal-rate compression in upcoming quarter; new Security Incident developments.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings evidence is self-aware: Blackbaud acknowledges AI sentiment can drive stock volatility independent of fundamentals. Core business is multi-year nonprofit subscription contracts (40/30/30 renewal cohorts across 2026-2028) with donor data spanning 20+ years and tax-exempt compliance workflows. Gemini smart speaker has zero overlap. Move -4.43%, five-day -5.54% on normal volume. Basket rule: same Google Home trigger cluster forces exploratory. 6% sizing.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case cites Blackbaud's own filing language warning that AI sentiment can drive volatility independent of fundamentals, but does not address whether that disclaimer is forward-looking risk language or backward-looking acknowledgment of actual customer churn or renewal compression.
red_team.critique: The setup is sound—a Gemini smart speaker does not compete with nonprofit fundraising software. However, the bull case leans heavily on Blackbaud's explicit AI-sentiment disclaimer as validation, when that disclaimer may actually indicate the company has already observed or modeled adverse customer sentiment in renewal negotiations. The five-day decline of -5.54% on near-normal volume (1.01x) is modest, but the bull case does not disclose whether the upcoming earnings will show any renewal-rate compression or SAC changes in the nonprofit segment.

entries.name: OneSpan Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker doesn't authenticate banking customers or sign legal documents. OSPN's regulated authentication and e-signature business is bound to PSD2/eIDAS standards. The move is small but on lighter volume, classic indiscriminate basket selling.
entries.what_kills: A major bank publicly choosing an AI-native authentication vendor over OSPN; FIDO Alliance changes that disadvantage OSPN.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings evidence is clear: regulated authentication (FIDO2/passkey standards) and e-signatures bound to PSD2/eIDAS standards—not a generative AI competitive concern. Gemini smart speaker cannot authenticate banking or sign legal documents. Move -3.72%, five-day -2.83% on below-average volume (0.87x). Classic indiscriminate basket selling. Basket rule: same Gemini trigger, forces exploratory tier. 6% sizing.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case correctly identifies the product-market separation but does not address whether the Build38 acquisition closing (March 2026 per the flag) has introduced execution or integration risk that a sympathy fade might surface prematurely.
red_team.critique: The mechanical thesis is sound—Gemini cannot authenticate or sign documents. However, the move is small (-3.72% on 0.87x volume) with a five-day change of -2.83%, which is within normal noise for a regulated-software company. The bull case does not flag whether Build38 integration timelines or banking-customer feedback on the acquisition could explain the move better than indiscriminate AI sympathy.

entries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: No government CIO is replacing court records of record with a Google Home speaker. TYL's moat is FedRAMP/state-specific compliance and entrenched multi-decade municipal relationships. The move on very light volume (0.65x) is classic indiscriminate AI fade.
entries.what_kills: A government-specific AI-native competitor winning a flagship state contract; FedRAMP changes that advantage AI-native vendors.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings acknowledge AI as a competitive and operational risk requiring TYL to innovate—but core business is government procurement with year-long sales cycles and decades-long municipal contracts. No government CIO is replacing court records with a Google Home speaker. Move -3.54% on very light volume (0.65x). Basket rule: same Gemini trigger, forces exploratory. 6% sizing.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case dismisses the move as indiscriminate AI fade on 'very light volume' (0.65x), but does not address whether government procurement sentiment on AI-native compliance tools or FedRAMP pathway changes might explain the move in advance of official announcements.
red_team.critique: The moat argument is structurally sound—no government CIO replaces court records with a consumer device. However, the five-day decline of -4.99% on very light volume (0.65x) is small enough to be explained by normal daily volatility rather than meaningful alpha. The bull case does not disclose whether any major state RFPs have recently shifted criteria toward AI-native compliance frameworks, which would materialize in TYL's forward guidance well before market awareness.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: Process-control metrology for advanced nodes is a direct beneficiary of AI-chip manufacturing complexity — more compute = smaller geometries = more inspection passes per wafer. A Gemini-on-Android release has no mechanical link to wafer fab inspection demand. The sharp drop on light volume looks like ETF-driven semi-cap sympathy with an already-extended five-day base.
entries.what_kills: Memory market downturn affecting Onto's specialty-semi exposure; delay or unfavorable terms on the Rigaku transaction; broader semi-cap capex air pocket.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings evidence minimal: AI mentioned only as general operational risk, core business is process-control metrology for advanced semiconductor nodes (direct beneficiary of AI chip manufacturing complexity). Move -5.9% on light volume (0.75x), but five-day change +15.37% (strongly positive) suggests the overreaction is sharp and may reverse quickly. Basket rule: same Android 17 trigger cluster forces exploratory. 6% sizing.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case argues that AI chip manufacturing complexity drives demand for ONTO's metrology, but the five-day change of +15.37% (strongly positive) contradicts the framing of a sharp overreaction requiring reversal—if the base is already extended and positive, timing and entry risk become material.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis that process-control metrology benefits from advanced-node complexity is sound. However, the data presents a puzzle: the move is -5.9% today on light volume (0.75x), yet the five-day change is +15.37%. This suggests ONTO was already extended prior to today's drop and the -5.9% move may be profit-taking rather than sympathy-driven. The bull case does not address whether memory-market weakness (which affects specialty-semi exposure) is the actual driver, or whether the Onto-Rigaku deal timeline adds execution risk that an AI sympathy fade might expose prematurely.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: AEIS's own 10-K identifies AI infrastructure as a material growth driver. Power conversion for fabs and AI data centers is structurally long the buildout that Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude inference all require. A consumer OS release has zero mechanical impact on data-center power-delivery demand. The sympathy drop on weak volume with a still-positive five-day looks like a clean overshoot.
entries.what_kills: Hyperscaler capex digestion narrative gaining traction; loss of design-win share to a competitor; Data Center Computing margins continuing to compress as that segment grows.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings evidence is explicitly supportive: 10-K states AI infrastructure investments are driving significant demand increases in Data Center Computing market; AEIS has accelerated capacity investments. Power conversion for data centers is structurally long the AI inference capex buildout. Move -5.45% on light volume (0.67x), five-day change +12.45% (strongly positive). This is a clean reversal setup. Basket rule: same Android 17 trigger, forces exploratory despite threat_assessment='none'. The stronger filings evidence (AI as stated growth driver, not threat) combined with positive five-day backdrop make this an attractive exploratory entry, but basket discipline holds sizing to 6%.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case claims AEIS's 10-K explicitly identifies AI infrastructure as a growth driver, making an AI-news sympathy drop 'mechanically backwards,' but does not verify that statement against the actual 10-K disclosure or address whether 'data-center power-delivery margin compression' could be the real driver masked by sympathy framing.
red_team.critique: The core thesis—power conversion for AI data centers is a tailwind, not a headwind—is plausible and the five-day change of +12.45% supports it. However, the bull case does not cite specific 10-K language or provide quantified data-center growth metrics to substantiate the claim that AI is a stated demand driver. The -5.45% move on 0.67x volume is modest, and if hyperscaler capex digestion narratives are genuinely gaining traction, AEIS margin compression from that segment growth could justify the move regardless of AI sympathy.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: DOCN's SMB/indie-developer cloud is orthogonal to a consumer Android+Gemini release. Their AI inference offerings (GPU droplets, GenAI platform) actually align them with the trend. The mechanical link from 'Google ships Gemini in Android' to 'DigitalOcean's SMB cloud subscriptions decline' is nonexistent.
entries.what_kills: Hyperscaler price-cuts on small instances making DOCN's pricing edge less meaningful; AI/ML revenue traction failing to materialize in next quarter; SMB churn acceleration.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings evidence frames AI/ML as growth opportunity, not competitive threat. SMB/indie-developer cloud is orthogonal to consumer Android+Gemini. Move -3.92%, five-day +3.48% (positive) on very light volume (0.52x). Basket rule: same Android 17 trigger forces exploratory tier. Confidence=3 (at minimum threshold) means this is a 6% test position to learn whether SMB cloud + GenAI inference can sustain through the sympathy fade.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case is marked confidence=3 (minimum threshold) and asserts SMB cloud is 'orthogonal' to Android/Gemini, but does not address whether hyperscaler SMB pricing pressure or consolidation of developer infrastructure onto integrated AI platforms could materially displace DOCN's indie-developer base.
red_team.critique: The setup is sound—a consumer OS release has no direct mechanical link to SMB cloud subscriptions. However, confidence=3 signals the bull case itself is at the threshold, and the critique does not address the real long-term risk: if Google, AWS, and Azure embed AI inference into their developer consoles, indie developers may consolidate onto one platform rather than maintaining separate DOCN subscriptions. The five-day change of +3.48% is positive but modest, and the -3.92% move on 0.52x volume is noise. The bull case does not disclose DOCN's customer churn metrics or DNE expansion rate trends.

entries.name: nCino, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's new 'AI Mode' on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
entries.thesis: nCino sells loan-origination workflow software to regulated banks where examination compliance, multi-year deployments, and integration with core banking systems form structural moats. A Meta consumer AI Mode for Facebook has zero plausible blast radius into bank loan-origination software. The negative move is pure thematic SaaS-AI sympathy.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that a major bank customer is replacing nCino with an in-house AI/LLM-based workflow; deterioration in subscription bookings; renewal rate decline.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3, threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings acknowledge AI as both competitive risk and growth strategy; nCino has integrated AI into banking workflow platform. However, core moat remains deep integration into regulated FI workflows (multi-year deployments, examiner-grade audit trails). Meta consumer AI Mode for Facebook has zero plausible connection to loan-origination software. Move -3.79%, five-day -1.99% on light volume (0.6x). Confidence=3 (minimum threshold) and threat_assessment='indirect' (not 'minimal' or 'none') argue for exploratory sizing. Basket rule applies (Meta trigger, along with LRN from same event). 6% sizing.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case is marked confidence=3 and threat_assessment='indirect' (not 'minimal' or 'none'), suggesting the bear case for AI-driven displacement in regulated banking workflows may have merit that the bull case acknowledges but downplays.
red_team.critique: The mechanical thesis is sound—Meta's AI Mode for Facebook has no plausible connection to loan-origination software. However, the threat_assessment='indirect' signals unresolved risk: if major financial institutions begin piloting LLM-based compliance, audit, and workflow automation in-house, NCNO's competitive position could erode even if a specific Meta product is not the direct threat. The five-day decline of -1.99% on 0.6x volume is small, but the bull case does not address whether nCino's own AI/ML integration is keeping pace with bank appetite for generative workflow automation. An indirect threat to regulatory-software switching costs is genuine even if not proximate.

entries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A consumer Gemini speaker has no overlap with payroll tax compliance, but PAYC's filings do reveal a real second-order AI risk (employee headcount compression at customers). Move is partial — five-day decline already extended. The Google Home trigger specifically is not the right driver; this is broader AI-on-HR-software anxiety.
entries.what_kills: Material guide-down on per-employee pricing or client headcount; a major HR-AI agent launch from Microsoft/Google specifically targeting SMB payroll; further breakdown of the already-extended 5-day chart.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3, threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='partial'. Unlike VRRM/MANH/BLKB, PAYC filings reveal a real second-order AI exposure: employee headcount compression at customers directly reduces per-employee payroll solution revenue. The Google Home trigger is a mis-match (payroll tax compliance, not HR automation), but the underlying anxiety is legitimate. Five-day decline already -9.51% on 1.73x volume (heavy)—sympathy fade is already extended and may not be unjustified. Confidence exactly at minimum threshold (3). Recommend WATCH rather than BUY: the panic_calibration='partial' and indirect threat mean the downside repricing may continue independent of this specific trigger.

entries.name: Paylocity Holding Corporation
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: Same logic as PAYC — Google Home doesn't file 941s or handle state UI registrations. However the move is on top of an already-extended 5-day decline, suggesting the broader HR/payroll AI fear is in motion. Sympathy fade is mis-targeted to this specific Gemini trigger.
entries.what_kills: An AI-native payroll competitor gaining material SMB traction; client headcount compression; further leg down on rising volume.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3, threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='partial'. Same logic as PAYC: HR/payroll AI exposure is real (employee headcount risk), but Google Home is the wrong trigger. Filings language is generic ('new technologies may deliver solutions more efficiently'). Five-day decline -9.97% on normal volume suggests the broader HR-AI fear is genuine and pricing in. Like PAYC, the panic is not entirely unjustified. Confidence=3, threat_assessment='indirect' pushes this to WATCH, not BUY.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: The Google Home speaker trigger is unrelated to language learning, but DUOL has real (though manageable) exposure to consumer AI products. The dopamine-loop/streak moat is the defense, and DUOL is monetizing AI via Duolingo Max. Move on light volume looks more like consumer-AI basket fade than fundamental threat.
entries.what_kills: A flagship consumer AI tutor product (ChatGPT Tutor, Gemini Tutor) gaining material DAU traction; declining DAUs or streak metrics; mobile platform fee or policy changes from Apple/Google.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3, threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='partial'. DUOL has real consumer AI exposure (filings explicitly flag GenAI as competitive threat to language learning category). However, DUOL has launched Duolingo Max (GenAI product) and the moat is dopamine-loop/streak mechanics—not language content. Confidence=3, threat_assessment='indirect' (not 'none' or 'minimal') means this is borderline. Move -3.65% on light volume (0.94x), five-day change +1.21% (positive) despite the flag—suggests market may not price the threat as highly. WATCH is appropriate: the panic is partially justified (real AI tutor competition exists) and the move is not as overshooting as true unjustified panic.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: A consumer OS release adding Gemini features to Android phones has zero mechanical connection to photomask demand. If anything, more AI compute = more advanced-node chip designs = more photomask demand. The selloff is pure thematic semi-sympathy tape with the sign flipped — Photronics is a beneficiary of AI capex, not a victim of a consumer mobile feature release.
entries.what_kills: An actual cyclical downturn in semi capex unrelated to today's trigger; loss of a top-two customer; further Section 232 / China export restrictions that materially reduce PLAB's addressable Asia revenue (82% of total).
entries.reasoning: PLAB is already a portfolio position (6 days held, +6.13% P&L). Screen 1 does not support ADD; new flag on the same name triggers no position action (already evaluated in position_decisions above, HOLD remains intact).

entries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: HCAT has plenty of real issues (DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, multiple restructurings, end-market funding cuts) but a consumer Android Gemini release is not one of them. HIPAA, BAA requirements, and EHR integration depth prevent general-purpose AI agents from touching provider data. The drop is sympathy-fade on top of an already-pressured name. Confidence intentionally limited because HCAT has genuine, name-specific issues that make this less clean.
entries.what_kills: Further Medicaid funding cuts pressuring health-system IT spend; client losses to Epic/Oracle accelerating; Ignite migration causing more revenue churn than guided.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 2 — below the screen's min_buy_confidence threshold of 3. Additionally, threat_assessment='indirect' and panic_calibration='unjustified', but filings explicitly cite Epic/Oracle/Optum/IBM as pre-existing competitors; the AI-driven sympathy is layered on top of genuine name-specific headwinds (DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, multiple restructurings, funding cuts). At confidence=2, this fails the entry bar.

entries.name: Stride, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's new 'AI Mode' on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
entries.thesis: Stride's revenue is contractually tied to per-pupil state funding for accredited virtual public schools — parents enroll children for diplomas, transcripts, and state-funded education, none of which a Meta Facebook AI feature can deliver. The sympathy-fade premise ('AI tutors replace edtech') is the wrong frame; Stride is a regulated school operator, not a tutoring app. The move appears to be an extreme overreaction conflating Stride with consumer edtech apps.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of state legislatures cutting virtual charter funding, charter non-renewals, or material enrollment declines in the next data release; a concurrent negative catalyst we missed.
entries.reasoning: LRN is already a portfolio position (6 days held, -12.86% P&L, on Meta AI Mode catalyst). Today's fresh flag on LRN (from the Meta trigger event) is a re-flag of the same name. No new BUY; position decision (HOLD) already made above.
